tier_definitions.IMPLEMENTED: The named behavior exists in the source tree and has direct component tests; this tier does not claim scientific or deployment validity.
tier_definitions.VALIDATED: Retained evidence exercises the named behavior under the explicitly stated synthetic, fixture, or in-process conditions; validity does not extend beyond those conditions.
tier_definitions.DEPLOYMENT_QUALIFIED: Independent retained evidence from the target deployment establishes the claim. Galadriel 0.9.0 intentionally has no claims in this tier.
tier_definitions.NOT_CLAIMED: The release makes no affirmative claim. The limitations field states the missing evidence or excluded scope.

claims.claim: The pure core validates finite per-observation NIS inputs and produces evidence-neutral magnitude verdicts with typed invalid-input failures.
claims.scope: galadriel-core source behavior under its validated configuration and bounded inputs
claims.limitations: Implementation does not authenticate physical truth, identify attack cause, or establish a calibrated false-alert rate.

claims.claim: Cross-channel correlation uses only sequence-aligned producer-declared common projections with matching frame, context, and frozen-prior provenance.
claims.scope: galadriel-core projection extraction and default correlation path
claims.limitations: Producer-attested means a provenance claim; cryptographic authenticity exists only when an independently configured transport binds publisher identity.

claims.claim: The NCP adapters bound JSONL/live state, validate versioned producer and lifecycle envelopes, and retain explicit typed terminal faults.
claims.scope: galadriel-ncp component and in-process receiver behavior at the exact NCP 0.8 pin
claims.limitations: No external actual-binary mTLS/ACL campaign or NCP 1.0 conformance is claimed.

claims.claim: The checked-in synthetic and fixture test suites pass on the recorded Rust 1.89 macOS arm64 baseline.
claims.scope: exact baseline commit, lockfile, toolchain, commands, seeds, fixtures and in-process transports recorded by the audit manifest
claims.limitations: A passing component suite is not independent field validation, a performance guarantee, or evidence for untested platforms.

claims.claim: Galadriel output is advisory evidence that cannot grant or widen authority under the machine-testable consumer-effect contract.
claims.scope: galadriel-core advisory authority model and documented consumer contract
claims.limitations: No downstream consumer adapter is included or qualified in 0.9.0.

claims.claim: Galadriel establishes sensor truth, attack intent, or a calibrated posterior probability.
claims.limitations: Consistency can be preserved by coordinated or moment-matched compromise; every release output remains calibrated_posterior=false.

claims.claim: The default profile meets a deployment false-alert, detection, latency, or availability acceptance threshold.
claims.scope: operational, field, mission and safety performance
claims.limitations: Existing retained evidence is diagnostic and includes unacceptable repeated-look false alerts and missingness abstention.

claims.claim: Galadriel 0.9.0 is qualified on NCP 1.0 or on a released pid-rs 1.x artifact.
claims.limitations: The selected NCP dependency is immutable v0.8.0; the pid-rs revision is immutable but lacks an upstream v1 release tag/artifact.

claims.claim: Crebain, Haldir, Prisoma or Paper2Brain provides a deployment-qualified Galadriel 0.9.0 integration.
claims.limitations: Only the Crebain reciprocal component fixture exists; no accepted recorded projected stream, downstream adapter, or secured deployment campaign exists.

claims.claim: Galadriel 0.9.0 crates are published on crates.io or carry a production-support promise.
claims.limitations: All workspace packages remain publish=false; 0.9.0 is a GitHub research source release.

claims.claim: Galadriel 0.9.0 has a project DOI or Zenodo record.
claims.limitations: The author will add DOI/Zenodo metadata only after this 0.9.0 release.

claims.claim: The 0.9.0 candidate has completed independent human, statistical, or security review.
claims.limitations: The retained review is author-operated and machine-assisted. Review packets and a blank external-comment interface are provided, but no independent person is represented as having reviewed or approved the candidate.

claims.claim: Per-assessment significance controls provide calibrated sequential or mission-wide error control across repeated windows, tracks, and operating time.
claims.scope: sequential monitoring, repeated looks, serial dependence, and mission-level multiplicity
claims.limitations: The release controls stated within-assessment channel and projection-axis families only. It does not convert those thresholds into an anytime-valid, track-wide, or mission-wide false-alarm guarantee.

claims.claim: At least one retained holdout arm uses an independently authored generator or external representative deployment dataset.
claims.scope: scientific holdout independence and external validity
claims.limitations: The checked-in generators and reciprocal Crebain fixture are repository-controlled compatibility inputs, not an independently authored or representative deployment holdout.

claims.claim: The complete platform/profile matrix and long-duration operational resilience envelope are qualified.
claims.scope: cross-platform equivalence, sustained resource behavior, cancellation, restart, scheduling, and availability
claims.limitations: Qualification covers the recorded author host, Linux CI, bounded hostile-input campaigns, and supported feature graphs; it is not a full platform matrix or a long-duration deployment soak.

claims.claim: An independent clean-room party reproduced the build, evidence, and release artifacts.
claims.limitations: The isolated-worktree qualifier is author-operated. It records exact inputs and deterministic artifacts but is not organizationally or procedurally independent.

claims.claim: Publication, rollback, revocation, withdrawal, and post-publication response were rehearsed against a disposable production-equivalent release environment.
claims.limitations: The release includes checked procedures and performs the actual GitHub publication path, but destructive rollback, credential revocation, and production-equivalent incident drills are intentionally not fabricated.
